Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0xE224 "SCEPTREOFBAGHDAD-E000-E224" -Di_irq_exit=0 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 lda #$35 sta $01 jsr $e000 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Sceptre of Baghdad.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added an init routine at $E000 which: 1. Turns off interrupts from the two CIA chips and acks any pending CIA interrupts. 2. Copies 1024 bytes of data from $C000 down to $0000 so that the dump can start at $0400. 3. Sets up the correct VICII settings. 4. Restores 24 bytes of data to $C3E8 from $E050 which are needed for correct in-game display. 5. And finally jumps to the entry point at $E224 (it couldn't use RTS because of the stack being cleared).
Notes: 1. This game is actually frozen, but it doesn't matter in this case, as the entry point used is after the initial background colour setup menu has been displayed. 2. The low memory dump data ($0000 - $0400) stored between $C000 - $C400 for use in step 2 above replaced the screen memory used to display the background colour setup menu. 3. The memory between $E000 and $E0C0 originally contained the code for the background colour setup menu, so could be safely replaced with the init routine and data at $E050.
